ucs.title: [LLM] Windshift / Bahamut (G0112) WindTail macOS dropper: quarantined .app spawned from Archive Utility with outbound HTTP beacon
ucs.description: Hunts the WindTail (a.k.a. WINDTAIL/Bahamut) macOS backdoor delivery chain unique to Windshift in the Middle East: a phishing link drops a ZIP whose embedded .app is auto-decompressed by Archive Utility and launched via a registered custom URL scheme, then beacons over HTTP. This combination of macOS targeting + Archive-Utility-spawned ad-hoc-signed bundle is a Windshift-specific signature, distinguishing it from generic Mac adware.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.os="macOS" AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("Archive Utility","BOMArchiveHelper","Safari","Mail","Finder","Google Chrome") AND (Processes.process_path="*/Users/*/Downloads/*.app/Contents/MacOS/*" OR Processes.process_path="*/private/tmp/*.app/Contents/MacOS/*" OR Processes.process_path="*/Volumes/*.app/Contents/MacOS/*") AND (Processes.signature="" OR Processes.signature="adhoc" OR Processes.signature_verified=false) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true values(All_Traffic.dest) as c2 values(All_Traffic.url) as urls from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="curl" OR All_Traffic.app="python" OR (All_Traffic.dest_port IN (80,443) AND All_Traffic.bytes_out<2048 AND All_Traffic.url="*.php*") by host | rename All_Traffic.* as *] | where lastTime-firstTime < 600 | table firstTime host user process_name parent_process_name proc_path c2 urls
ucs.kql: let suspectApps = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where DeviceType =~ "Mac" or InitiatingProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has "Apple" | where FolderPath matches regex @"\.app/Contents/MacOS/" and (FolderPath startswith "/Users/" or FolderPath startswith "/private/tmp/" or FolderPath startswith "/Volumes/") | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("Archive Utility","BOMArchiveHelper","Safari","Mail","Finder","Google Chrome","firefox","Microsoft Edge") | where isempty(ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName) or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName !has "Apple" | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessId; let beacons = DeviceNetworkEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where RemoteUrl has_any (".php","/checkupdate","/get/","/string/","/usr/") or (RemotePort in (80,443) and InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("curl","python","python3")) | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessId; suspectApps | join kind=inner beacons on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.InitiatingProcessId | where datetime_diff('minute', NetTime, Timestamp) between (0 .. 30) | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP
ucs.rationale: Windshift is one of the very few APTs that has consistently weaponized native macOS backdoors (WindTail/WindTape) against Middle Eastern targets. Objective-See's analyses (Wardle, Dec 2018 / Jan 2019) document the exact chain modeled here: ZIP delivered via spearphishing link, decompressed by Archive Utility, executed via custom URL scheme handler, ad-hoc signed bundle stored under ~/Downloads, th

ucs.title: [LLM] Windshift / Bahamut spearphish-to-persistence: VBScript launched by Office/browser writes Run-key auto-start
ucs.description: Detects the Windows side of the Windshift kill chain documented by SANS (Aug 2018): spearphishing email or link drops a VBScript/JScript stager that is executed via wscript.exe from a user-writable temp path, then registers a Run / RunOnce key pointing back at the same script for surveillance persistence. The temporal correlation of Office/browser → wscript from Temp → Run-key write within minutes is the actor-specific signature.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as procTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe") AND Processes.parent_process_name IN ("outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe") AND (Processes.process="*.vbs*" OR Processes.process="*.vbe*" OR Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.jse*") AND (Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Users\\Public\\*") by host Processes.user Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as regTime values(Registry.registry_value_data) as run_value values(Registry.registry_key_name) as run_key from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.action="modified" AND Registry.registry_path="*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*" AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*.vbs*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*.vbe*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*wscript*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*cscript*") by host | rename Registry.* as *] | eval delta=abs(regTime-procTime) | where delta < 1800 | table procTime regTime delta host user parent cmdline run_key run_value
ucs.kql: let stager = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe") | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("outlook.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","explorer.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (".vbs",".vbe",".js",".jse") | where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\(AppData\\Local\\Temp|AppData\\Roaming|Downloads|ProgramData|Users\\Public)\\" | project ProcTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessId; let persist = DeviceRegistryEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet" | where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce") | where RegistryValueData has_any (".vbs",".vbe","wscript","cscript") | project RegTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData; stager | join kind=inner persist on DeviceId | where datetime_diff('minute', RegTime, ProcTime) between (-30 .. 30) | join kind=leftouter (EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(14d) | where DeliveryAction == "Delivered" | project EmailTime=Timestamp, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, NetworkMessageId) on $left.AccountName == $right.RecipientEmailAddress | project ProcTime, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine, RegistryValueData, SenderFromAddress, Subject
ucs.rationale: Windshift's Windows surveillance chain documented by SANS ICS (Karim, Aug 2018) and follow-on Bahamut reporting hinges on simple but distinctive components: VBScript/JScript stagers under user-writable directories, executed via the standard wscript host, with persistence via classic Run/RunOnce keys — not scheduled tasks, services, or WMI subscriptions favored by louder crews. Correlating an Offic
